pages.text:
gliders taking off. At one time there were 11 of them.

   After tea in the van we drove William & Thomas back to school & set off home, arriving at 6 pm

__Sat October 25th__

   All 7 of us set off for London at 10.45. We dropped off the church news leaflet at St George's & left William's racket at the Priory to be restrung. At [?20] 1.45 we arrived at the Lee Valley Leisure Centre __Picketts Lock__. **** It has had several smart new wooden buildings put up since the last time we were here. John & Charles are thrilled with the new playground. There is an ominous mouldy smell.

   We had lunch & Eve made coffee while Simon & William finished putting up the 2 tents. The reason for the mouldy smell was discovered — 5 large coffee mugs full of old, old black berries.

   We clambered on board the bus for the Tottenham Hale underground station. twenty

five minutes later... we clambered off again, when Simon suddenly realised that we were driving away from the sun. Fortunately another bus just happened to be passing. Thomas ran & flagged it down. Nearly an hour later we passed the campsite, & 15 minutes later we were on the train bound for __Oxford Circus__.
	Again we made a pilgrimage to Liberty's, Fabrics & lighting. We lost Peter briefly. Then we spent nearly an hour in Hamleys. John bought quirky stuff that blows raspberries. Charles bought a Porshe car. Peter bought a hand-shaker trick toy. We went into Next & Burberrys, bought a Coke & set off back to the campsite. Thomas bought tickets for a movie. Pizza hut supper.
	Eve put John & Charles to bed while Simon took T, W, P to the movie. Cold.

__Sunday October 26th__
	Gas wouldn't flare. Simon changed to the red cylinder. Heavy frost on the tents.
				The clocks changed, so we were able to leave at 9.30., catching 2 buses to 7 Sisters & a tube train to Embankment. A marvellous trip down the river on a boat in bright, bright sunshine, with an amusing guide. The boat stopped at Tower Bridge & we circled in front of the Queen's House before docking. It was very beautiful. __Greenwich__
	We visited St. Alphege's Church, with

captions: The ball is raised at 12.58 every day, + dropped at 1.00. precisely, so that boats on the Thames know the time.
pages.text:
its stained glass window to Thomas Tallis. The church felt rather like St. Paul's in the Jewelry Quarter - wide + open, designed by Hawksmoor.

    We went across into Greenwich Market - bustling, crowded, [?led] in by Christian Halstead. John bought a board game, Peter some posters + Eve a belt.

    We had lunch from some booths - hamburgers + Thai woked stuff.
    Then we set off up the Hill to the Old Observatory. Most of us went around the Observatory. Charles went round the slop

[caption below drawing:] The ball is raised at 12.58 every day, + dropped at 1.00. precisely, so that boats on the Thames know the time.

Flamsteed did endless calculations. John Harrison spent years perfecting a nautical clock, to help calculate longitude accurately. It was later used by Captain Coo[k]
    Simon + Thomas continued to look at the Observatory while the rest of us rushed down the hill to see the "Cully Sark" 1868, last of the great Tea Clippers which also carried wool.
    She had a crew of only 28 men. Carrying wool was incredibly lucrative. The wool cost £1000. Shipping charges were £4000. The cargo, back in England, was worth $100,000.
    Sail area was 32,000 sq. feet. She is 280 feet long.
    At 3.45 we started for home, on the Docklands light railway, after first walking under the Greenwich Tunnel which was built for workmen.
    We reached the campsite in time to

buy a coke & hotdog before the movie started at 5.45. "Contact".

      We went back to the van for Auntie Olive cake & hot chocolate before bed. Thomas, William & Peter set off to see another movies.

__Monday October 27th__
      (It is now Tues am. & John has just got this book out of the glove compartment. It is icy cold.) Monday was overcast. Sunday night was slightly warmer than Saturday night, but it is still very cold & the heater is broken. In [?the] bed I wear the goosedown jacket, a padded wristcoat around my hips, & an aran cardigan across my feet.
      We first visited the Imperial War Museum. Simon looked at the planes & SOE. Eve looked at the Trenches with John & Charles & Belsen, & code breaking. We all looked at the tanks. Eve forgot to tell Thomas & Peter what time we were to leave, but we all finally met up at the Tate with Robert Upex for lunch. The dining room was busy until 2.30., so we

looked around the wonderful Clore Gallery full of Turners. He was the son of a barber. Simon looked at Eric Gill sculptures - reliefs of the wind for the Tube station - female figures on lines.
      (The milk in the fridge is frozen).
      The lunch was delicious. Parsnip soup/ (with lemon in it) vegetable terrine. Silverside of beef & onion tart, [?for] lemon pie / mango sable. The waitress made a mistake with the bill, charging £100 too little. Simon thought she was the best waitress we had had.
      Charles & Eve looked at some old English paintings. Thomas & Peter went off shopping. We set off for Tottenham Hale. We stopped at Euston to pick up supper. William went to "Face", Peter (who missed the bus) went to "my best friend's wedding", Simon took John & Charles to "Hercules", & Thomas & Eve saw "Air Force One".
      After hot chocolate, we went to bed, but T & W watched another movie, "Full Monty".

Tues October 28
Freezing cold night. Milk frozen in fridge. After taking down the 2 tents, we left at 9.45. Thomas has washed + dried breakfast each day in return for sleeping in his own tent.
     We reached Madame Tussauds at 10.30. to meet Rob, Elizabeth + Cattarine Hungley in the queue. But the queue was 1¼ hours long so we left them to walk to Regents Park instead.

[glued-in animal cut-outs: snail, red-and-black frog, elephant]

We saw horrendous snakes, amusing chimpanzees + beautiful giraffes. John + Charles got lost after going on the way back from the giraffe bouncy castle, so we were late meeting Rob + the girls in Regents Park. (Thomas had been shopping + bought a fleece.) We searched for somewhere to eat, Topkapi, which was prepar[d]
